message:
test(ffs-core): NUL-name regression on btrfs unlink/rmdir

Continue the bd-48wuw embedded-NUL regression suite onto the
btrfs side of ffs-core. The previous test commit covered
rename/link/symlink on both the FsContext and ops surfaces; this
fills in the two btrfs mutating ops that weren't yet pinned:

- btrfs_write_unlink_invalid_nul_name_rejected
    Create a regular file `unlink_nul_src.txt` under the btrfs
    root, attempt unlink with `b"unlink\0nul.txt"`, assert
    FfsError::Format and verify the original file still resolves
    to its original ino.

- btrfs_write_rmdir_invalid_nul_name_rejected
    Create a real directory under the btrfs root, attempt rmdir
    with `b"...\0..."`, assert FfsError::Format and verify the
    target directory still exists.

Both go through the high-level `ops: &dyn FsOps` dispatch so the
test exercises the same code path the FUSE bridge does, not just
the inner FsContext API.

Plus one tiny rustfmt fix in mknod_socket where an `.expect(...)`
call had drifted out of indented alignment after a recent edit:

  -        )
  -        .expect("mknod socket");
  +            )
  +            .expect("mknod socket");

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
